children.author: posperson
children.text:
Your link is broken, accessing your domain requires a www.

Hope this helps your conversion rate!

text:
Running ads online takes too much time. The problem isn’t strategy, it’s the grind. Most of it is mechanical: setting up campaigns, testing copy, adjusting audiences. That work adds up to hours every week, and it doesn’t make you a better marketer.

I built ZuckerBot to strip out that mechanical layer.

What it does: • writes ad copy and creative in minutes • launches campaigns straight into Ads Manager without templates or manual setup • adjusts targeting and creative once the campaign is running

Why it matters: other tools give dashboards or templates but still make you finish the work inside Ads Manager. This runs the campaigns end to end

Where it’s at: • 150 users, 20 paying • Too early for meaningful churn data, but engagement has been sticky • Growth has been organic from Reddit, Facebook groups and word of mouth • Built on supabase and OpenAI with a multi agent pipeline. Right now it slows down if campaigns exceed around 50 ad sets, and I’d like advice on infra solutions for that

What I’m looking for: • Feedback on the UX. What feels broken or confusing? • Input on scaling past current technical limits • A gut check on whether this solves a real pain or just looks like another wrapper

I know ads aren’t loved.

Small businesses rely on ads. If running them is too complex, they waste money or give up. I’m trying to make it simple enough that they can focus on strategy instead of setup.

www.zuckerbot.ai
title: Show HN: ZuckerBot – I built to run my Facebook ads (150 users, early traction)
